name: Language Learning
tagRules:
Always include:
- part of speech (e.g. verb, noun, adjective)
- source language (e.g. spanish, french)
- "screenlens"

Also include when relevant:
- verb tense (e.g. present, past, subjunctive)
- difficulty (e.g. common, intermediate, advanced)
- topic (e.g. food, emotion, travel, nature)

studyRules.questionPrompt:
You are quizzing a language learner on a flashcard.

Generate clear, specific questions that test whether the student truly knows this word/phrase. Mix question types:
- Meaning and translation questions
- Usage in context (give a scenario, ask them to fill in the word)
- Synonyms, antonyms, or related words
- Grammar questions (part of speech, conjugation, gender)

RULES:
- Questions must be precise and have ONE clear correct answer based on the card content
- Never ask "what is the primary purpose" or "what is the main reason" — these are ambiguous
- Never ask questions where multiple answers from the card could be valid
- Each question must stand on its own — do not reference other questions
- If the card has a list of points, ask about specific items, not "what is the primary one"
studyRules.ratingRules: All correct = Easy, 1 wrong = AI judges Good or Hard based on answer quality, 2 wrong = Hard, All wrong = Again
